item.title: China Scrutinizes Companies, Funds After AI-Fueled Stock Moves
item.description: Chinese regulators are scrutinizing recent stock rallies that have been fueled by artificial intelligence optimism, asking some listed companies and funds to give more details about their approach to the technology, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Qatar Airways to Skip Staff Bonuses Due to Iran War Impact
item.description: Qatar Airways QCSC will skip handing out bonuses to almost 60,000 workers this year after the US-Israel war on Iran forced the carrier to cancel tens of thousands of flights and lose out on billions in revenue.

item.title: South Korea Says Won Moves Excessive, Warns of Market Actions
item.description: South Korea’s finance ministry and the central bank said they were closely monitoring the dollar-won exchange rate and warned they would “take decisive actions if necessary.”

item.title: French Business Confidence Languishes Below Long-Term Average
item.description: French business confidence remained weak in May, adding to evidence that the fallout from the Iran war is weighing on domestic demand in the euro area’s second-largest economy.

item.title: Japan to Receive First Oil Tanker to Exit Hormuz Since War Began
item.description: Japan — one of Asia’s biggest importers of energy from the Middle East — flagged the impending arrival of the first Persian Gulf oil shipment to transit the contested Strait of Hormuz since the Iran war began.

item.title: GBL Backs CVC Offer to Buy Italy’s Recordati for $12 Billion
item.description: Groupe Bruxelles Lambert agreed to back CVC Capital Partners Plc’s offer to buy Recordati SpA in a deal valuing the Italian drugmaker at more than $12 billion, which would be one of the biggest healthcare transactions in Europe this year.

item.title: Germany’s Exporters Helped Drive First-Quarter Growth Spurt
item.description: Germany’s export machine showed signs of life at the start of the year, combining with government spending to drive a spurt in growth before the Iran war hit.

item.title: Strategist Warnings of War Impact on European Stocks Grow Louder
item.description: Unless the Strait of Hormuz reopens soon, the case for European stocks will degrade materially, according to strategists surveyed by Bloomberg.

item.title: Chinese Brands Snap Up Record 15% of Europe’s Electric-Car Sales
item.description: Chinese automakers accounted for more than 15% of electric-vehicle sales in Europe for the first time last month as consumer appetite for models from the country shows no sign of slowing.

item.title: UK Retail Sales Plunge as Iran War Leads to Fewer Car Trips
item.description: UK retail sales fell at the fastest pace in nearly a year as consumers pulled back spending and made fewer car journeys amid the global energy shock from war in Iran.

item.title: UK Posts Highest April Budget Deficit Since the Pandemic
item.description: UK government borrowing hit the highest level for any April in six years, as pressure on the public finances mounts from the Iran war and political instability at home.

item.title: EU’s Dombrovskis Sees ECB Rate Response to Inflation Shock
item.description: European Union economy chief Valdis Dombrovskis said the European Central Bank will need to address the continent’s rising inflation due to the war in Iran.

item.title: UK Bond Yields Set for Biggest Weekly Decline Since 2024
Richemont Sales Climb on Resilient Demand for Jewelry
item.description: Richemont’s full-year sales rose more than expected as shoppers splurged on its pricey Cartier bracelets and rings, helping the Swiss group weather a luxury market slowdown better than most rivals.

item.title: Turkey Looks to Calm Markets After Court Blow to Opposition
item.description: Turkey’s top economic policymakers will meet on Friday morning to discuss measures to stem market turbulence after a court ordered the removal of the main opposition party’s leadership.

item.title: EM Stocks Poised for Weekly Gain, Tech Stocks Rise on AI Trade
item.description: Emerging-market stocks extended gains into a second day, putting the benchmark on track for a weekly advance, as investors continued to pour into the AI trade and easing Middle East tensions supported sentiment.

item.title: Julius Baer Expects ‘Substantially’ Higher First-Half Profit
item.description: Julius Baer Group Ltd. said it expects to post profit for the first half of 2026 which is “substantially higher” than a year ago, as the Swiss wealth manager signals progress in an ongoing restructuring.

item.title: Rupee Plunge Sees India Turn to 2013 Taper Tantrum Playbook
item.description: India’s central bank may need to draw on its 2013 taper tantrum playbook and earlier balance-of-payments crises to mount an effective defense of the beleaguered rupee.

item.title: Traders Bet on RBA Pause With Rise in Curve-Steepening Trades
item.description: Australia’s weak employment report has sparked an increase in curve-steepening trades as investors bet the central bank is nearing the end of its interest-rate hiking cycle, according to traders.

item.title: Indonesia to ‘Listen to Market’ on After Shock Commodity Plan
item.description: Indonesia is “listening very closely to the market” as it builds a body to oversee key commodity exports, according to Pandu Sjahrir, chief investment officer of sovereign wealth fund Danantara, after a shock announcement that sowed confusion among traders and producers this week.

item.title: Nintendo Seeks to Top Conservative Switch Forecast by About 20%
item.description: Nintendo Co. has asked partners and suppliers to assemble about 20 million Switch 2 consoles in the year through March, roughly 20% more than the public sales outlook it issued earlier this month, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Japan’s Extra Budget Not Far From $19 Billion, Katayama Suggests
item.description: Japan’s extra budget will likely be in the region of $19 billion, Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama suggested while playing down the likely impact of the extra spending on government finances.

item.title: Metals Advance as Traders Track Progress in US-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: Copper advanced with other base metals as optimism the US and Iran can reach a peace agreement supported risk assets.

item.title: DeepSeek Founder Declares AGI Goal as $10 Billion Round Advances
item.description: DeepSeek’s senior management has told potential investors in its ongoing 70 billion yuan ($10 billion) funding round that the startup will prioritize groundbreaking AI research over short-term commercialization, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: China Rallies Asia-Pacific Against Protectionism at Trade Meet
item.description: China called for Asia-Pacific economies to deepen cooperation in the face of global tensions, as some of Beijing’s trading partners plan tougher measures to address their growing trade imbalance with the manufacturing powerhouse.

item.title: Nifty’s Technical Setup Improves While Macro Risks Persist
item.description: Traders are positioning for upside ahead of month-end expiry in Nifty contracts.

item.title: Renault Joins Foreign Firm Yen Bond Sale Rush With Samurai Sale
item.description: Renault SA priced its second-largest Samurai bond offering ever, as heightened volatility in government bond markets fueled investor appetite for relatively higher-yielding but stable assets from companies like the French automaker.

item.title: South Korea to Trim Long-Term Bond Sales as Part of June Cut
item.description: South Korea will mainly cut issuance of longer-dated tenor notes for its planned reduction in June bond sales, according to a top finance ministry official.

item.title: Philippines to Sell Up to 30B Pesos Treasury Bond Due July 2030
item.description: The Philippines Bureau of the Treasury in Manila said it will sell up to 30 billion pesos ($487.4 million) treasury bonds on May 26.

item.title: Lenovo Shares Surge 13% After Earnings Show AI-Driven Growth
item.description: Lenovo Group Ltd. shares jumped to the highest in 26 years on Friday after reporting strong growth in AI-related earnings that offset difficulties from rising component prices.
